screens.name: Songs library
screens.purpose: Keep Goodbye readable in the existing songs workspace while reflecting that the chart was cross-checked against the provided MP3.

ux_rules: Reuse the current songs workspace and score viewer without layout changes.
Express audio uncertainty in notes and published lead-sheet metadata rather than inventing new UI chrome.
Prefer the musically cleaned chart over a noisy raw MIR dump.

screen_contexts.layout_intent: Keep the existing list-detail songs surface intact while making the new source evidence readable inside the current metadata and notes areas.
interaction_risks: Audio-derived confidence could be overstated if the notes sound too definitive.
Lead-sheet publication could imply approval that the run does not actually claim.
notes: This task changes content, not UI structure.

constraints: Confine product-code edits to song content and publish storage.
Do not replace the chart with raw audio bars.
open_decisions: A future symbolic source could upgrade the draft lead sheet to approved status.

critical_judgments: Goodbye remains readable and selectable in the existing songs workspace.
The source and chart notes clearly communicate that the MP3 was validation evidence, not definitive engraving.

visual_constraints: No new visual treatment is required.
